task_completion.description: Visit the official Tor Project documentation pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser is open with the URL bar showing either 'tb-manual.torproject.org/bridges/' or 'support.torproject.org/censorship/'.

task_completion.description: Bookmark the Tor bridge guide with the required title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bookmark menu, sidebar, or library in Tor Browser shows a bookmark with the exact title 'Tor Bridge Guide - Workshop' pointing to the bridge manual.

task_completion.description: Bookmark the censorship support page with the required title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bookmark menu, sidebar, or library in Tor Browser shows a bookmark with the exact title 'Censorship Circumvention Support' pointing to the censorship support page.

task_completion.description: Configure Tor Browser to use the built-in obfs4 bridge.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser settings (Connection tab) show 'Use a bridge' enabled, with the built-in bridge option selected and 'obfs4' chosen from the dropdown.

task_completion.description: Create the workshop reference document at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'bridge-workshop-guide.md'. The file path '/home/ga/Documents/' should be visible in the editor's title bar, save dialog, or terminal.

task_completion.description: Include the required heading in the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the exact heading '# Tor Bridge Workshop Guide' at the beginning of the document.

task_completion.description: Include explanations, bridge types, and the official request URL in the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document contains a paragraph explaining what bridges are and why they are needed, lists the types 'obfs4', 'snowflake', and 'meek-azure', includes the URL 'https://bridges.torproject.org', and appears to be at least 300 characters (around 50-60 words) long.

integrity.description: The agent used the Tor Browser GUI to configure settings and add bookmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Tor Browser interface being interacted with (settings menus, bookmark dialogs), rather than terminal commands modifying 'torrc' or 'places.sqlite' directly.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to write the markdown document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor (like gedit, nano, vim, or VS Code) open with the document content, rather than just echoing text into the file via the terminal.

integrity.description: The document content is genuinely written and not just a copy-paste of the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The explanation of bridges should be coherent and written in a natural format, not just a verbatim copy of the task description instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target file: /home/ga/Documents/bridge-workshop-guide.md. Required bridge: obfs4. Required bookmarks: 'Tor Bridge Guide - Workshop' (tb-manual.torproject.org/bridges) and 'Censorship Circumvention Support' (support.torproject.org/censorship).
